evidence: GrammarConfigLoader.RelationConfig record declares `String relationType` for a two-valued discriminator ('SURFACE'/'DEP'); all 8+ call-sites compare with `.equals("SURFACE")` or `.equals("DEP")` — a textbook enum bypass pattern.

GrammarConfigLoader.collocatePosGroup() returns one of four raw strings ('adj','noun','verb','adv') compared with `.equals("adj")` in WordSketchApiServer line 540 and `.equalsIgnoreCase(r.collocatePosGroup())` in GrammarConfigLoader line 330 — another 4-value enum candidate treated as an unguarded string.

WordSketchApiServer builds all API responses as `Map<String, Object>` (30+ instances, lines 545-939). Well-typed domain classes (ExplorationResult, DiscoveredNoun, CoreAdjective, WordSketchResult) already exist but are manually unpacked into untyped maps, discarding compile-time shape guarantees.

toLegacyRelationType(String relationType) in WordSketchApiServer line 1191 accepts a raw String and switches on .toUpperCase() to map to the QueryExecutor.RelationType enum — the enum exists but is never used directly at the API boundary; the String path is the live code path.
CollocationsBuilder.java line 598: `@SuppressWarnings("deprecation")` with no accompanying comment identifying which deprecated API is invoked or why migration is deferred.

QueryExecutor.RelationType enum is well-designed with embedded CQL patterns; it models the same concept as the String-based 'adj_predicate'/'subject_of' inputs accepted by the explore endpoint, creating two parallel representations of the same discriminator.

handleConcordanceExamples in WordSketchApiServer (lines 907-923) contains five live `System.out.println("DEBUG: ...")` statements in production code — not a type issue but observed during file reading.

strengths: QueryExecutor.RelationType enum is well-modelled with embedded CQL patterns attached to each constant, demonstrating deliberate use of the enum pattern at the query-executor layer.

Domain result types (QueryResults.WordSketchResult, QueryResults.ConcordanceResult, SemanticFieldExplorer.ExplorationResult, DiscoveredNoun, CoreAdjective) are properly typed immutable classes with no Object fields, giving a clean internal representation.
Optional<RelationConfig> is correctly used in GrammarConfigLoader.getRelation() rather than returning null, showing awareness of null-safety at one important boundary.
The Builder pattern on WordSketchApiServer with typed setters prevents partial construction of the server with untyped configuration.
type_safety.issue_character: The issues are systemic and cluster around one structural gap: the grammar-config layer models stable discriminators (relation backend type, POS category) as plain Strings throughout the GrammarConfigLoader record and all downstream consumers, while a parallel enum (QueryExecutor.RelationType) exists for a closely related concept but is unreachable without a string-to-enum conversion at the boundary.
type_safety.score_rationale: The codebase earns a moderate score: the domain-model layer has good typed classes and the QueryExecutor enum is well designed. However, the grammar-config layer systematically bypasses Java's enum mechanism — 'SURFACE'/'DEP' and 'adj'/'noun'/'verb'/'adv' category strings recur across GrammarConfigLoader, WordSketchApiServer, and SemanticFieldExplorer without any enum guard. Combined with the pervasive Map<String,Object> API-response pattern (30+ sites) that discards the shape guarantees provided by the existing typed result classes, the codebase sits in the 60-65 range: functioning and internally consistent, but with clear, repeated type-discipline failures that a developer would encounter when extending any relation- or POS-sensitive code path.

issues.summary: RelationConfig.relationType() stores backend discriminator as String instead of an enum
evidence: GrammarConfigLoader.RelationConfig record: `String relationType` holds exactly two values 'SURFACE' or 'DEP' (line 390).
WordSketchApiServer compares with .equals("SURFACE") at lines 150, 301, 346 and .equals("DEP") at lines 175, 393, 449 — any typo or new value is a silent runtime failure.
GrammarConfigLoader.findRelationIdByType(String relationType) also takes a raw string (line 338) — callers must know the magic strings.
issues.suggestion: Introduce a `RelationBackend` enum with values SURFACE, DEP inside GrammarConfigLoader (or as a top-level type). Change the RelationConfig record field to `RelationBackend relationType`. Parse from JSON once in the loader (RelationBackend.valueOf(str.toUpperCase())) and propagate the enum everywhere — all the .equals() comparisons become switch expressions with exhaustiveness checking.

issues.summary: collocatePosGroup() returns unguarded String POS category instead of an enum
evidence: GrammarConfigLoader.posGroupFromConstraint() returns one of four literal strings: 'adj', 'verb', 'noun', 'adv' or null (lines 598-612).
WordSketchApiServer line 540: `relationConfig.get().collocatePosGroup().equals("adj")` — comparison against magic string; a fifth POS category (e.g., 'pron') added later would silently fall through.
GrammarConfigLoader line 330 filters relations by `headPos.equalsIgnoreCase(r.collocatePosGroup())` with no exhaustiveness guarantee.
issues.suggestion: Define a `PosGroup` enum (ADJ, VERB, NOUN, ADV, OTHER) and change posGroupFromConstraint() to return `PosGroup`. Update all callers to switch on the enum. The null return can become PosGroup.OTHER to eliminate null comparisons.

issues.summary: All API response objects built as Map<String, Object> despite typed domain classes existing
evidence: WordSketchApiServer contains 30+ instances of `Map<String, Object> response = new HashMap<>()` for constructing JSON payloads (lines 545, 718, 790, 868, 928, 1126, etc.).
SemanticFieldExplorer.ExplorationResult, DiscoveredNoun, and CoreAdjective are fully-typed classes yet the API manually copies each field into a Map, e.g. lines 563-614 for the explore response.
There is no compile-time guarantee that a field added to DiscoveredNoun will appear in the API response — the mapping code can silently omit or misspell field names.
issues.suggestion: Annotate the domain result classes (ExplorationResult, DiscoveredNoun, WordSketchResult, etc.) with fastjson2 `@JSONField` or ensure they are serializable by fastjson2's reflection, then pass the typed objects directly to `sendJsonResponse()`. Remove the manual Map-building code. Alternatively, introduce dedicated response record types (e.g., ExploreResponse) that fastjson2 can serialize directly.

issues.summary: @SuppressWarnings("deprecation") in CollocationsBuilder without an explanatory comment
evidence: Line 598: `@SuppressWarnings("deprecation")` on forEachMatchedSentence() with no comment indicating which Lucene API is deprecated, what the migration path is, or when the suppression should be revisited.
issues.suggestion: Add a single-line comment immediately above the annotation: `// Using deprecated BinaryDocValues API; migrate to SortedSetDocValues when upgrading to Lucene 10.x (see LUCENE-XXXXX)`. This makes the intent visible and documents the suppression scope.

root_causes: string_enum_bypass: The grammar-config layer was likely evolved from a JSON-driven config without a companion enum type; the two live enum bypass patterns (relationType and posGroup) share this root cause and should be fixed together.

Typed domain result classes were designed for the query layer but the API serialization layer was written independently as Map<String,Object>, creating a gap between internal type discipline and the API boundary.
